_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to develop, update, and/or maintain standard operating procedures (SOPs).
reference_document_element_description: Develop and maintain operational documentation for data systems, including standard operating procedures, runbooks, escalation matrices, and knowledge base articles.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI OP domain. OP-T-010 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-A-001 (RC, cosine: 0.511), LS-S-007 (LS, cosine: 0.511).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.0/10 (raw 0.700). Shared vocabulary: [develop, maintain, operating, procedures, standard]. DCWF-only terms [ability, sops, update] vs WIDAI-only terms [articles, base, data, documentation, escalation, knowledge] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.865 (P=0.868, R=0.861). Balanced P/R — the 10-word DCWF element and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Ability to develop, update, and/or maintain standard operating procedures (SOPs)" and "Develop and maintain operational documentation for data systems, including stand" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.529 < STS 0.700 (gap -0.172). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [ability, sops, update] and [articles, base, data] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 26.1%. Shared tokens: [develop, maintain, operating, procedures, standard]. DCWF-only: [ability, sops, update]. WIDAI-only: [articles, base, data, documentation, escalation, knowledge].
